solution:
def breakPalindrome(self, palindrome: str) -> str:
    for i in range(len(palindrome)):
        if palindrome[i] != palindrome[len(palindrome)-1-i]:
            palindrome[i], palindrome[len(palindrome)-1-i] = palindrome[i]^2, palindrome[len(palindrome)-1-i]^2
    return palindrome

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The algorithm iterates through the string once using a for loop. It checks if the characters at corresponding positions are not equal. If they are not equal, it squares both characters and swaps them. This ensures that the resulting string is no longer a palindrome and is lexicographically smallest. The time complexity is O(n) because the algorithm iterates through the string once. The space complexity is O(1) because the algorithm only uses a constant amount of space. The energy implications are low because the algorithm does not require any additional energy-consuming operations.
